answer: Property taxes in Jackson County are due on October 15 each year. You can pay online at jacksongov.org/taxes or in person at the County Collector's office.

To appeal your property assessment, you must file a written appeal with the Board of Equalization within 30 days of receiving your assessment notice. Contact the Assessment Department at (816) 881-3530.
Voter registration in Missouri closes 28 days before an election. You can register online at sos.mo.gov, by mail, or in person at any license office or the Election Board.

To request an absentee ballot, submit an absentee ballot application to the Jackson County Election Board at least one week before the election. Applications are available at jacksongov.org/elections.
Building permits in unincorporated Jackson County are issued by the Development Services department. Submit your application at jacksongov.org/permits. Processing takes 5-10 business days.
A home occupation permit is required if you run a business from your home in unincorporated Jackson County. Annual fee is $35. Apply online or at 415 E 12th Street, Kansas City, MO 64106.
Kansas City Water Services handles water and sewer billing. To report an outage or water main break, call (816) 513-0567 any time, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
The Jackson County Courthouse is located at 415 E 12th Street, Kansas City, MO 64106. Office hours are Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, except on official county holidays.
